segments.text: Jesus went unto the mount of Olives.
And early in the morning he came again into the temple, and all the people came unto him; and he sat down, and taught them.
And the scribes and Pharisees brought unto him a woman taken in adultery; and when they had set her in the midst,
Master, this woman was taken in adultery, in the very act.
segments.speaker: Scribes and Pharisees

segments.text: Now Moses in the law commanded us, that such should be stoned:
but what sayest thou?
segments.speaker: Scribes and Pharisees

segments.text: This they said, tempting him, that they might have to accuse him. But Jesus stooped down, and with his finger wrote on the ground, as though he heard them not.
So when they continued asking him,
he lifted up himself, and said unto them,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And again he stooped down, and wrote on the ground.
And they which heard it, being convicted by their own conscience, went out one by one, beginning at the eldest, even unto the last: and Jesus was left alone, and the woman standing in the midst.
When Jesus had lifted up himself, and saw none but the woman,
he said unto her,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: No man, Lord.
segments.speaker: Woman

segments.text: And Jesus said unto her,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then spake Jesus again unto them,
saying,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Thou bearest record of thyself; thy record is not true.
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: Jesus answered and said unto them,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Where is thy Father?
segments.speaker: Jews

segments.text: Jesus answered,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: These words spake Jesus in the treasury, as he taught in the temple: and no man laid hands on him; for his hour was not yet come.
Then said Jesus again unto them,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Will he kill himself? because he saith, Whither I go, ye cannot come.
segments.speaker: Jews

segments.text: And he said unto them,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Who art thou?
segments.speaker: Jews

segments.text: And Jesus saith unto them,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They understood not that he spake to them of the Father.
Then said Jesus unto them,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As he spake these words, many believed on him.
Then said Jesus to those Jews which believed on him,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: We be Abraham's seed, and were never in bondage to any man: how sayest thou, Ye shall be made free?
segments.speaker: Jews

segments.text: Jesus answered them,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Abraham is our father.
segments.speaker: Jews

segments.text: Jesus saith unto them,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: We be not born of fornication; we have one Father, even God.
segments.speaker: Jews

segments.text: Jesus said unto them,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Say we not well that thou art a Samaritan, and hast a devil?
segments.speaker: Jews

segments.text: Jesus answered,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now we know that thou hast a devil. Abraham is dead, and the prophets; and thou sayest, If a man keep my saying, he shall never taste of death.
segments.speaker: Jews

segments.text: Art thou greater than our father Abraham, which is dead? and the prophets are dead: whom makest thou thyself?
segments.speaker: Jews

segments.text: Jesus answered,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Thou art not yet fifty years old, and hast thou seen Abraham?
segments.speaker: Jews

segments.text: Jesus said unto them,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then took they up stones to cast at him: but Jesus hid himself, and went out of the temple, going through the midst of them, and so passed by.
